title: polypodium brackenridgei
intro: Polypodium brackenridgei, also known as Brack's polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, derived from its leaves and rhizomes. The health benefits of Polypodium brackenridgei include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, among others. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making a decoction or infusion from its leaves and rhizomes, which can be used to create teas, tonics, or other medicinal products.
